source: local-cache (canonical · Medicare Therapeutic Shoes for Persons with Diabetes — LCD L33369)

criteria.text: The patient has diabetes mellitus and is under a comprehensive plan of care for diabetes.
criteria.quote: the patient has diabetes mellitus and is under a comprehensive plan of care for diabetes

criteria.text: At least one qualifying foot condition. Peripheral neuropathy alone does NOT qualify — it must be accompanied by callus formation.
criteria.quote: at least one of the following conditions: previous amputation of the foot or part of the foot; a history of previous foot ulceration; a history of pre-ulcerative calluses; peripheral neuropathy with evidence of callus formation; foot deformity; or poor circulation
